description: Trigger discrimination: full-tier shard/resume architecture vs tier selection (risk-tiered-validation-selector), per-commit local mirroring (local-ci-mirror-preflight), whole-pipeline design (ci-pipeline-architect), flake diagnosis (flaky-test-detective), and test-suite automation architecture / runner choice (qa-automation-architect).

cases.prompt: Split our full validation into named functional shards with a persisted status file, resume-after-timeout, a catch-shard for unassigned tests, and one aggregate required check in CI.
cases.reason: The full tier's internal architecture — shards, status, resume, aggregate gate — is exactly this skill; no tier is being selected, no local mirror run, and the whole pipeline is not being redesigned.

cases.prompt: Decide from the changed files whether this PR runs docs-only, fast, or full validation — fail closed on anything unmatched.
cases.reason: Choosing the tier is the selector; the shard machinery only defines what the FULL tier consists of once selected.

cases.prompt: Before I commit, run everything CI will run locally — baseline against clean main first and classify any failures.
cases.reason: Executing checks per commit with baseline and failure classification is the preflight; where shards exist it invokes the shard runner, but the request is a run, not an architecture.

cases.prompt: Overhaul our CI/CD: stage ordering, artifact build-once, deploy gates with named human approvals, and secret handling.
cases.reason: The delivery pipeline's stage graph, gates, and secret governance are ci-pipeline-architect; shard design is one validation stage's internals and hands its wiring there.

cases.prompt: One spec fails intermittently in the ui-e2e shard with the same assertion — root-cause it.
cases.reason: Diagnosing an intermittent test is flaky-test-detective; the shard design must never retry or resume flakes into green.

cases.prompt: Structure our whole test codebase, pick the runners, and wire the suites into CI with sensible tiers and retries.
cases.reason: Structuring the whole test codebase, choosing runners, and wiring suites into CI is qa-automation-architect. This skill designs only the internal shard/resume/aggregate machinery of one already-selected validation tier, not the suite architecture or runner choice.
